source: Deep research (May 2026) — Pi normie ran out of Groq TPD after 228 turns/day
title: Route normie mode through Cerebras (primary) with Groq as fallback
what_failed: Normie mode used Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile exclusively. Free-tier TPD is ~100k tokens, exhausted in heavy-chat days. Switching providers was a config-only change because Cerebras is already wired in app/config.py and core/providers/cerebras.py — but never used in the normie path.
where_failed: _respond_normie called self.groq.chat.completions.create directly with no provider fallback.
why_likely: Cerebras integration shipped in T-048 (LLMRouter) but normie mode predated the router and never adopted multi-provider routing.
suggested_fix: Instantiate self.cerebras = OpenAI(api_key=CEREBRAS_API_KEY, base_url=https://api.cerebras.ai/v1) at agent init. In _respond_normie: try Cerebras first (model llama-3.3-70b, 1M TPD free, 3-5x faster, 16-bit precision), fall through to Groq on any failure. Log used_provider for telemetry.
